content.full_text:
RULES COMMITTEE CRITERIA

A mass mailing will be "political" if the sender (candidate, elected official or committee):

a) attacks an opponent or a political party

b) makes reference to an upcoming election (including ballot measures)

c) polls voters on candidates

d) discusses issues without fairly presenting both sides. He may, however, make simple, unelaborated position statements.

e) uses pictures except in letterhead

f) mails to only one party or faction

g) mails material from which one can reasonably conclude that his primary purpose is name identification

h) discusses his political philosophy, background or qualifications
